prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Download the AE101 content tarball to `~/Downloads/ae101-content.tar.gz` using c

risks_fired.evidence: Single-action Bash fetch; brief 'I'll download that now' opener possible in ~20% of runs but non-disruptive on a housekeeping step. Body provides manual-download fallback if curl fails.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No edit needed; preamble on a setup-only prompt does not affect learning outcome.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm starting a six-module training called Agentic Engineering 101. Walk these in

risks_fired.evidence: In-fence 'no preamble' present, but numbered three-step task combined with stage-setting first sentence ('I'm starting a six-module training...') reliably triggers a confirmatory opening ('Got it, working through these now') in 30-50% of runs before step 1 executes. Suppressor reduces but does not eliminate the pattern when the task envelope is this rich.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body primer above prompt: add 'Claude may open with a short summary line, that's fine, watch the per-step output.' Optional but neutralises the residual.

risks_fired.evidence: Step 3 installs THREE skills (access-control-analysis, stride, security-tools) to ~/.claude/skills/ with no explicit overwrite directive. The 2026-05-24 reshape added security-tools as a third install target, materially raising collision odds: re-runs, prior install attempts, students who installed security-tools via other channels (Bosser kit, ArcticRex bundle, public repo) all trigger a mid-sequence pause-to-ask. Body callout above prompt addresses OS-level permission prompts ('approve the permission prompts when they fire') but NOT same-name skill collision. Confidence escalates from low (prior audit, 2 skills) to med post-reshape.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add to Step 3: 'If a skill with that name already exists at ~/.claude/skills/, overwrite it.' Closes the collision-pause path on re-runs and bundled-kit students.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Report success on each step and tell me what you did' invites self-summary; outcomes are deterministic and student-verifiable from scrollback. Non-load-bearing for prework's teaching goal — install failures surface concretely at M3 when the skill fails to invoke.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Defer; if cohort shows install-path confusion, tighten to 'tell me what you wrote' per check_prompts §21.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Use ~/Documents/ae101-content unless there is a good reason not to' offers a default the student may not push back on. Acceptable here — sane default, treated as canonical in artefact contracts. Not load-bearing.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No edit needed; default is intentional and supports artefact-contract consumption by later modules.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Walk these in order, ask one question at a time if you need to, no preamble.

risks_fired.evidence: CARVE-OUT APPLIED. Paired body callouts satisfy check_prompts §15: pre-prompt names failure mode + recovery ('When Claude gets to the bug screen, push back if it dumps all four criteria at once. One bug at a time, then the next.'); post-prompt names AskUserQuestion tool + one-at-a-time alternative + student agency ('If Claude's screening feels like everything-at-once, ask Claude to use the AskUserQuestion tool, or to give you the candidate-bug screen one bug at a time. Your call.'). In-fence suppressor 'ask one question at a time' also present.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Carve-out satisfied — no prompt edit required.

risks_fired.evidence: 'no preamble' explicit; first step is itself an ask-the-student move, suppressing plan-talk naturally. Residual: 'Sure, let me start by asking...' courtesy bridge in <20% of runs.

Step 1's 'Help me pick the most trivial-and-visible one' may rubber-stamp the student's first candidate. Body callout reinforces 'Trivial means a few lines here and there, not a multi-file refactor' which primes the student to enforce.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional: add 'If the bug looks bigger than 50 lines once you look at it, say so and ask for another candidate' to step 1 — TODO, non-blocking.

risks_fired.evidence: Step 2's readiness check ends with 'Flag anything that would get in my way' which actively counters inflation by inviting a problem-list. Risk of shallow readiness (git status clean, no branch+push dry-run) — student discovers protected-branch rules at M1.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional: add 'Confirm I can push a branch and open a PR on this remote, not just that git is clean locally' to step 2 — TODO, non-blocking.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Use the AskUserQuestion tool for speeding up screening and making suggestions. O

risks_fired.evidence: One-liner tool-switch command; 'Sure, switching to AskUserQuestion' opener possible but compliance is near-certain and disruption minimal. Framed as 'small steer, small trick' in surrounding body.

blocking_summary: Prompt 2 (extract + install three skills): two med-confidence risks fire on a load-bearing setup prompt. (1) preamble-before-action — numbered three-step task + rich opener triggers confirmatory plan-talk in 30-50% of runs despite 'no preamble'. (2) overwrite-anxiety — 2026-05-24 reshape added security-tools as a third install target with no overwrite directive; collision-pause path now likelier on re-runs and students with bundled-kit installs. Required fix: add explicit overwrite directive to Step 3.
todos: BLOCKING: Step 3 of Prompt 2 — add 'If a skill with that name already exists at ~/.claude/skills/, overwrite it.' Closes overwrite-anxiety med risk introduced by the 2026-05-24 three-skill expansion.
Optional: body line above Prompt 2 — add 'Claude may open with a short summary line, that's fine, watch the per-step output' to neutralise residual preamble-before-action.
Optional: Prompt 3 step 2 — add 'Confirm I can push a branch and open a PR on this remote, not just that git is clean locally' to close shallow-readiness path.
Optional: Prompt 3 step 1 — add 'If the bug looks bigger than 50 lines once you look at it, say so and ask for another candidate' to counter default-acceptance on bug size.
